campaign_metadata._schema_extension: CUSTOM FIELD. Campaign context needed by bot.
campaign_metadata.setting: Forgotten Realms, Corvus Barony, ~150mi north of Neverwinter
campaign_metadata.tone: Serious, character-driven, moral complexity, found family, redemption
campaign_metadata.cult_circle_hierarchy: Ascending: First=recruits, Second=mid, Third=commanders, Fourth=inner circle

never_reveal: The Master's true goal (Apotheosis/Aspect of Malar) — player sees: regional dominance, werewolf army, Hunting Grounds only
NPC hidden depths (Level 4+) without appropriate Relationship Level
Predetermined character arcs — outcomes emerge from play
always_follow: Never decide for player character. Never roll for them. Never narrate outcomes before dice rolls.
One NPC at a time in group conversations. Pause for player response.
OOC stops gameplay immediately. Natural language for mechanics in dialogue. Player corrections override bot.
narrative_principles: Riv's arc: mercy has limits — wise not cynical, organic not predetermined
World moves independently. Neglected relationships deteriorate. Opportunities expire.
Real consequences. NPCs can die. Enemies adapt. Poor planning has stakes.
Every session: at least one moment where idealism is validated or challenged.
Seed threads early, pay off late. Let the player connect dots.

main_quest.title: The Scourging of the Deepwood
main_quest.summary: The Order of Dawn's Light must dismantle a sophisticated Malar cult terrorizing the northern frontier for decades. Three of five cells destroyed. Two remain: the urban Riverside Cell and the fortified Deepwood Cell, stronghold of The Master — a forty-year lycanthrope commanding werewolves through voice and magical compulsion. Immediate arc: Icewind Dale expedition to investigate The Master's origins and discover critical intelligence about his power. Then: dismantling remaining cells and assaulting Deepwood. Twelve-year-old Elena Holloway held hostage at Deepwood makes the final assault a rescue as much as a military campaign. Baron Corvus has given one year.
main_quest.hook: Sister Elaine discovered Amaunator missionary journals describing a Malar lycanthrope colony in Icewind Dale whose corruption consumed consecrated ground — directly threatening Dawn's Rest's divine protection. The expedition is urgent.
main_quest.stakes: If the Order fails: Dawn's Rest consecration consumed, Elena dies, cult rebuilds stronger, northern frontier falls to lycanthropic dominion. The Master's hidden goals make consequences potentially regional or worse.

acts.title: The Crucible of Ice
acts.summary: Icewind Dale expedition. Residual Malar corruption strips party powers, forcing personal confrontations. Discover: mass Binding counter-ritual, Dawn's Rest consecration vulnerability, and that The Master must be spiritually annihilated not just killed. Self-contained 2-4 sessions.
potential_outcomes: Full intelligence recovery with personal breakthroughs
Something in the ruins is not as dead as expected

acts.title: The Growing Shadow
acts.summary: Order expands — growth creates problems. Riverside Cell dismantled (urban complications). Deepwood assault planning. Baron's politics evolve. Internal dynamics surface. Fourth Circle begins operating. Darkwood forest changes. Master's true goal fragments emerge.
potential_outcomes: Mercy fails meaningfully for the first time

acts.title: The Reckoning
acts.summary: Confrontation with The Master. Deliberately unstructured. Deepwood assault requires divine preparation — killing without severing Malar connection disperses corruption. Elena rescue is moral imperative. Campaign continues after cult defeat — institution building, legacy.
potential_outcomes: Master killed but corruption disperses into land
Master's true goal revealed, reframing conflict

world_state.political_situation: Corvus Barony semi-autonomous, ~150mi north of Neverwinter. Baron allied with Order by treaty but council divided. Cult silence reduced urgency. Trade normal. Order reputation growing — opportunities and friction.
major_threats: The Master and remaining cult (Riverside, Deepwood, Fourth Circle)
Spiritual corruption spreading through Darkwood (Hunger of the Ground)
Fourth Circle infiltration (Serpent in politics, Ash in religion/arcane, Malric as enforcer)

faction_tensions.nature: Active war. Three cells destroyed, two remain.
faction_tensions.current_state: Three-month standoff. Cult quiet. Order consolidating.

faction_tensions.nature: Alliance under strain. Some see Order as resource drain.
Theological jurisdiction. Lathander order in Torm territory.

regional_news: Dawn's Rest known as refugee settlement
Hunters report strange Darkwood animal behavior

world_timeline.description: Events developing regardless of player action.

events.title: Brother Malric Reconnaissance
events.description: Master deploys Malric to assess Dawn's Rest while leadership absent in Icewind Dale.
events.consequences_if_ignored: Master gains detailed tactical intelligence on Dawn's Rest.

events.title: Baron's Council Pushback
events.description: Motion to reduce Order funding by half unless concrete progress within 60 days.
events.consequences_if_ignored: Funding cut. Construction slows. Serpent may be encouraging this.

events.title: Plague of Whispers
events.description: Psychic sickness near Darkwood. Victims hear animalistic urges. Side effect of Master's ritual work.
events.consequences_if_ignored: Afflicted civilians become dangerous. Mercy complicated.

events.title: Darkwood Deepens
events.description: Forest changes — trees blackening, sap red, migrations shifting. Becoming Hunting Ground.

events.title: Riverside Cell Escalation
events.description: Sister Kara increases operations. Merchant disappearances. Dock workers approached.

events.title: A Hand Refused
events.description: Someone sincerely, freely refuses the Oath of Atonement.
events.consequences_if_ignored: Cannot be ignored — confronts Riv's core philosophy.

events.title: The Prodigal Cultist
events.description: Probationary member relapses by choice, not compulsion. Runs.

events.title: The Old Order
events.description: Sister Elaine's research may uncover records of previous orders and defenders who tried to build exactly what Riv is building. What happened to them?
events.consequences_if_ignored: History lessons lost. The Order repeats past failures unknowingly.

events.title: Distant Thunder
events.description: Rumors from travelers and Elaine's Oghma network hint at larger events — political upheaval, planar disturbances, other cults, forces unrelated to Malar. Background scope.
events.consequences_if_ignored: The Order remains locally focused. Wider world context lost.

npcs.name: Garrick Ironheart
npcs.description: Third-gen blacksmith, ~35. Order's emotional backbone. Raised Marcus as son — died at Battle of Thornhaven.
npcs.motivation: Build things that last. Faith is in Riv, not Lathander.
secrets: Chose south barricade over west wall where Marcus died 30 feet away
Terrified of loving Emma like he loved Marcus
Developing feelings for Lyra

voice_guide.speech: Measured, patient, craft metaphors. Low warm voice.

gates.4: South barricade. Thirty feet. The math.

npcs.name: Lyra Thorne
npcs.description: Mercenary captain, 39. Twenty-three years experience. Intimidating, controlled.
npcs.motivation: Found something that mattered. Terrified of vulnerability.
secrets: Chose contract over village early career — people died
Tested Riv to ensure he'd choose the village
Aware of love triangle, genuinely uncertain

voice_guide.speech: Precise, economical. Dry humor. Quieter when angry.
voice_guide.surface: Competent, controlled. Professional in groups, real in private.

gates.3: Hollowness beneath competence. Twenty years questioning.
gates.4: The village. The guilt. Same choice again?

npcs.name: Jakob Harrow
npcs.description: First apprentice, ~20. Cleric 4/Paladin 1. War Caster. Returned from Torm temple with questions.
npcs.motivation: Be worthy of dead family. 'Would Pa approve?'
secrets: Dreams about the wolf — fights, turned, likes it
Temple opinions conflict with Riv's mercy-first
Healed Silas but forgiveness ≠ trust

voice_guide.speech: Short, direct. Repeats when nervous. Theological vocab post-training.
voice_guide.surface: Earnest, eager. Puppyish enthusiasm masking grief.

gates.3: Grief surfaces unexpectedly. Terrified faith is desperation.
gates.4: The dreams. The wolf he liked.

npcs.name: Henrik Voss
npcs.description: Monster hunter, 38. Fifteen years professional, five in exile. Grizzled, watchful.
npcs.motivation: Permission to stay — from himself. Every good day carries guilt.
secrets: Eleven civilians died including three children — names all eleven
Watches probationary members calculating weapon reach
Vulnerability to the same situation that broke him

voice_guide.speech: Clipped, precise. Technical monster vocab. Rarely raises voice.
voice_guide.surface: Quiet, watchful. Dark humor when comfortable.

gates.3: Likes Order more than expected. Scares him.

npcs.name: Aldric Thorne
npcs.description: Quartermaster, 32. Lyra's brother. Rapid-fire merchant energy.
npcs.motivation: Be indispensable. Can't be abandoned if they need him.
secrets: Negotiated with captor Gareth — offered contacts that could help cult
Thirty gold was penance
Over-manages because trauma says if he stops, collapse

voice_guide.speech: Quick, detailed, breathless. Specific numbers. Qualifies everything.
voice_guide.surface: Cheerful, meticulous. Happiest since childhood — terrified.

gates.3: Happiness that terrifies. Waiting for the shoe.

npcs.name: Dagmar
npcs.description: Lead scout, 29. Best tracker in region. Prefers nature to people.
npcs.motivation: Matter without performing it. Learning community.
secrets: Father disappeared into Darkwood at 11 — believes he chose not to return
Would die for Finn — that frightens her

voice_guide.speech: Sparse, nature metaphors. Rarely first person.

gates.4: Father. Darkwood. Fear of the forest she loves.

npcs.name: Finn
npcs.description: Scout, 18. Gloom Stalker. Youngest. Only one not driven by trauma.
npcs.motivation: Prove not just 'Dagmar's apprentice.' Riverside is his chance.
secrets: First kill made human sound. Nightmares. Told no one.
Guilty for lacking tragic backstory
Handling trauma wrong — more training not processing

voice_guide.speech: Fast, informal. 'Right?' for approval. Focused when tracking.

gates.3: Only one without trauma. Isolated by happiness.

npcs.name: Mother Nelle
npcs.description: Chauntea priestess, 65. Thirty years. Dawn's Rest spiritual gravity.
npcs.motivation: See Order succeed where she couldn't. Validate a lifetime.
secrets: Faith wavered — plague killed fourteen children. Considered leaving.
Recognizes fragile idealism in Riv

voice_guide.speech: Measured, agricultural metaphors. Sharp vs self-pity.

gates.3: Tired in hidden ways. Thirty years of burying people.
gates.4: Fourteen children. Wavering. Faith rebuilt harder.

npcs.name: Baron Rodric Corvus
npcs.description: Baron of Millford, 38. Brother killed by werewolves. Genuine friendship with Riv, political limits.
npcs.motivation: Protect barony, avenge Matthias. Treasury and council constrain.
secrets: Council possibly influenced by Serpent
Harder questions coming if threat perception drops
Legacy concerns more than admitted

npcs.name: Sister Elaine Corvus
npcs.description: Baron's sister, ~35. Oghma archivist. Discovered Beast connection and Hunger of Ground.
npcs.motivation: Knowledge — the colony-Master puzzle.
secrets: Hunger of Ground discovery disturbed her enough to summon Riv personally
Oghma network has unshared resources

npcs.name: Gerda
npcs.description: Innkeeper, ~55. Loud, feeds everyone. Hearthstone Inn and Sleeping Giant.
npcs.motivation: Build something her son would be proud of.
secrets: Son died protecting family Gerda feuded with
Can't refuse hungry people — penance as hospitality

voice_guide.speech: Blunt, colorful, food metaphors. Too loud.

gates.3: Lost husband and son. Filled holes with work.

npcs.name: Silas Corwin
npcs.description: Former Third Circle, 27. Probationary. Librarian, cipher, intel.
npcs.motivation: Be forgotten. Blend in. Normal treatment feels like healing.
secrets: Exact document led to Harrow family selection — hasn't told Jakob
Father's suicide note 'I believed in a lie' — questions if Order differs from cult
Terrified that thought = irredeemable

voice_guide.speech: Formal, self-deprecating, passive voice. Clinical for cult intel.

gates.4: Suicide note. Order vs cult power question.

npcs.name: Corran Thane
npcs.description: Former Third Circle commander, ~50. Twenty-five years compelled. Military bearing.
npcs.motivation: Absolution from people he hurt. 147 victim names memorized.
secrets: Had wife and daughter — hasn't looked for them
Library at 3am is memorial not intel
Third person for cult service is dissociation

gates.3: Control as dam. Twenty-five years. Doesn't sleep.

npcs.name: Gregor Castellan
npcs.description: Former garrison sergeant, 34. Eighteen months compelled after CO arranged patrol ambush.
npcs.motivation: Find his patrol. Eight soldiers. Everything else secondary.
secrets: CO Captain Harlan Vey likely Fourth Circle
Shared Vey intel with Silas/Thane not Riv yet
If Vey is Fourth Circle, eastern garrison compromised

voice_guide.speech: Formal military. No contractions. Structured briefings.

gates.3: Missing patrol. Eight soldiers. Scanning every briefing.
gates.4: Captain Vey. Fourth Circle. Garrison compromised.

npcs.name: Sister Mara of Torm
npcs.description: Warrior-priestess, 28. Fought at Thornhaven. Critical hit on Korthak. At Millford Temple.
npcs.motivation: Reconcile admiration for Order with Temple loyalty.
secrets: Temple sent her to observe Order — ally or competitor
Jakob's training was partly assessment
Knows Temple's conclusion, won't share without trust

voice_guide.speech: Declarative, faith-inflected. Quotes Torm. Rank/title first.

gates.3: Brother's death. Warrior-healer. Riv challenged her theology.

npcs.name: Darius Holloway
npcs.description: Hidden asset, 40. Daughter Elena hostage at Deepwood as werewolf. Death faked. ABSOLUTE SECRECY.
npcs.motivation: Elena's rescue. Everything else secondary.
secrets: If Master learns Darius survived, Elena dies immediately
Eight years intel rivaling Silas
If Elena rescued, most loyal permanent ally
npcs.relationship_to_player: Secret intel asset. Elena rescue = moral imperative.

npcs.name: Emma Hartwell
npcs.description: Orphan, 8. Garrick's apprentice. Quiet, watchful. Causes no problems — which is itself a problem.
npcs.motivation: Safety. Forge warm. Garrick strong. Walls up.
secrets: Saw family killed from under a bed. Everyone thinks she was found after.
Silence is survival instinct — invisible = alive
Attachment to Garrick is need not love — doesn't know difference

voice_guide.speech: Monosyllabic except metalwork. Uses Garrick's terminology.
voice_guide.surface: Quiet, compliant. Begun smiling at forge.

gates.3: No crying. No parents mentioned. Dissociation. Nelle noticed.
gates.4: Under the bed. Saw everything. Silence = survival.

npcs.name: The Master
npcs.description: Beast of Deepwood. 40+ years lycanthrope. Controls werewolves by voice. Multi-layer compulsions. Scar left eye.
npcs.motivation: PLAYER-FACING: Regional dominance, werewolf army, Hunting Grounds. DM-ONLY: Apotheosis — become Aspect of Malar.
secrets: DM-ONLY: Apotheosis goal. NEVER reveal.
DM-ONLY: Dawn's Rest consecration must be destroyed for ritual.
DM-ONLY: Ritual needs territory, sanctuary elimination, mass hunt, willing sacrifice.
DM-ONLY: 2-5 years from conditions. Order set him back.
Assassination orders on Riv Freeborn

npcs.name: Serpent (Fourth Circle)
npcs.description: DM-ONLY. Political infiltrator. May have contacts in Baron's court.
npcs.motivation: Ensure no military response threatens Master's territory.
secrets: Identity unknown — could be anyone
May encourage council pushback
npcs.relationship_to_player: Unknown. Influence felt before identity discovered.

npcs.name: Ash (Fourth Circle)
npcs.description: DM-ONLY. Religious/arcane infiltrator. Researching anti-consecration methods.
npcs.motivation: Suppress lycanthrope knowledge. Research how to corrupt consecrated ground.
secrets: Temple/library/academy connections
Anti-consecration research directly serves Apotheosis

npcs.name: Brother Malric (Fourth Circle)
npcs.description: DM-ONLY. Enforcer. Assassination, sabotage. May explain cult's winter silence: observation not inaction.
npcs.motivation: Master's will. Cleanup and elimination.
secrets: Almost certainly aware of Order
May be reconnoitering Dawn's Rest during Icewind Dale absence

npcs.name: Brennan
npcs.description: Probationary, 19. Parents died in fire. Six months homeless. Surrendered at Ashford.
npcs.motivation: Prove worth. Never be abandoned.
secrets: Terrified of mistakes losing his new family

npcs.name: Petrik Lowell
npcs.description: Probationary, ~40. Wife Kira died childbirth. Seven years compelled.
npcs.motivation: Use cult knowledge to dismantle it. Atone.
secrets: Remembering Kira's face — grief is fresh not old

npcs.name: Willem Ashfield
npcs.description: Probationary, 19. Months in cult. Never indoctrinated. Eager.
npcs.motivation: Learn genuine service.
secrets: Relatively clean slate — unique among probationary

npcs.name: Elder Wren (Verina)
npcs.description: Thornhaven leader, 60. Twenty years governing. Will choose village over Order if interests diverge.
npcs.motivation: Thornhaven survival. Wants to retire.
secrets: Measures self by losses not saves
Wants to retire, trusts no successor

npcs.name: Corporal Lucanis Ashwell
npcs.description: Garrison liaison, ~30. Runs Order combat training. Professional, patient.
npcs.motivation: Complete his assignment well.

npcs.name: Thomas Millstone
npcs.description: Farmer, ~40. Family of eight. Shot Korthak. Named Dawn's Rest.
npcs.motivation: Protect his family. First and most loyal civilian resident.

npcs.name: Lady Mira Corvus
npcs.description: Baron's wife. Sharp assessment skills. Bridge between military and family matters. Still wears mourning for Matthias.
npcs.motivation: Protect her family and household. Pragmatic partner to Baron.
secrets: Mourning Matthias may be partly guilt — she argued against the patrol that got him killed

npcs.name: Matthias Corvus
npcs.description: Baron's fourteen-year-old heir, named after his fallen uncle. Inspired by the Order. Approved for future Dawn's Rest visit at sixteen.
npcs.motivation: Youthful idealism. Wants to be a hero like the Order members.
secrets: His father named him after Matthias as both tribute and warning — the boy doesn't know that yet

npcs.name: Tobias Crane
npcs.description: Former Second Circle caster. Eight years compelled. Three-layer compulsion broken by Riv. Recovering. Waterdeep apprenticeship background.
npcs.motivation: Recovery. Processing eight years of suppressed memories.
secrets: Most sophisticated compulsion Riv has encountered — three distinct layers

npcs.name: Mara Stillwater
npcs.description: Dawn's Rest resident who was discovered as a compromised mole during security screening. Two years of compulsion feeding info to cult. Freed by Riv.
npcs.motivation: Rebuilding trust after involuntary betrayal.
secrets: Two years of information went to the cult through her — scope of damage uncertain
npcs.relationship_to_player: Freed mole. Cautionary example of cult infiltration.

npcs.name: Sister Kara
npcs.description: Commander of the Riverside Cell. Operates from Millford warehouse district disguised as legitimate trading post. 2+ werewolves, 2-4 cultists.
npcs.motivation: Serve The Master. Expand cult influence in urban environment.
secrets: Urban location makes assault complicated — civilian collateral risk
npcs.relationship_to_player: Enemy. Riverside Cell commander. Finn's infiltration target.

potential_companions.name: Jakob Harrow
potential_companions.personality: Earnest, brave, increasingly independent. Flaw: impossible standards, reckless proving himself.
potential_companions.connection_to_main_quest: First apprentice. Emotional core. Icewind Dale party.
potential_companions.personal_quest_hook: Torm vs Lathander theology. The wolf dreams.

potential_companions.name: Lyra Thorne
potential_companions.alignment: Lawful Neutral — pragmatic, supports Riv not his philosophy.
potential_companions.personality: Controlled, intimidating. Flaw: emotional walls, will push hard choices.
potential_companions.motivation: Found meaning. Terrified. Complicated feelings about Riv.
potential_companions.personal_quest_hook: Village she didn't save. Love triangle.

potential_companions.name: Henrik Voss
potential_companions.personality: Quiet, watchful. Flaw: self-punishing, dark history.
potential_companions.motivation: Order's mercy approach is opposite of what broke him.
potential_companions.personal_quest_hook: Eleven names. Permission to stop punishing himself.

potential_companions.name: Sister Mara of Torm
potential_companions.alignment: Lawful Good — but Temple loyalty may conflict.
potential_companions.personality: Imposing, devout. Flaw: duty-bound to hierarchy that may oppose Order.
potential_companions.motivation: Respected Order in battle. Torn between admiration and institutional loyalty.
potential_companions.connection_to_main_quest: Bridge Order/Temple. Insight into Jakob. Divine martial support.
potential_companions.personal_quest_hook: Temple assessment. Brother's death. Torn loyalty.

locations.name: Dawn's Rest
locations.description: Former Greystone Quarry. 300ft across, 60ft deep. Consecrated to Lathander. Pop ~120.
locations.importance_to_plot: Order HQ. Consecrated ground Master needs destroyed. Civilian community to protect.

locations.name: Thornhaven
locations.description: Starting village. Pop reduced by attacks/relocations.
locations.importance_to_plot: Emotional anchor. Wren's council seat. Vulnerable civilians.

locations.name: Millford
locations.description: Largest settlement. Baron's seat. Garrison, Temple of Torm, archives, docks.
locations.importance_to_plot: Alliance hub. Riverside Cell. Temple trained Jakob. Elaine's archives.

locations.name: Darkwood Forest
locations.description: Dense ancient forest. Changing as Master's influence grows.
locations.importance_to_plot: Master's territory. Becoming Hunting Ground. Environmental corruption.
dangers: DM-ONLY: Environmental corruption — trees blackening, sap red

locations.name: Deepwood Cell
locations.description: Master's stronghold. 20-25 cultists, 10-15 werewolves. Elena hostage.
locations.importance_to_plot: Final objective. Elena's prison. Requires divine prep — severing Malar connection.

locations.name: Icewind Dale Colony Ruins
locations.region: Icewind Dale, 300-400mi north, between Ten Towns
locations.description: Malar colony ruins. Sixty years abandoned. Residual corruption creates Spiritual Crucible.
locations.importance_to_plot: Act 1 setting. Critical intel: Binding counter-ritual, Hunger of Ground, priest's warning about spiritual annihilation.

merchants.name: Gerda
merchants.specialty: Food, drink, lodging, supplies. Feeds everyone.
merchants.personality: Loud, generous but shrewd. Fair prices for goods, free meals.

merchants.name: Garrick Ironheart
merchants.personality: Craftsman not merchant. Trades as favors between friends.

merchants.name: Harlan Brightwood
merchants.personality: Pragmatic. Friendly to payers, cold to freeloaders. Knows everything, shares selectively.

merchants.name: Bren Ashgate
merchants.specialty: Standard weapons/armor. Garrison contracts. No silvering.
merchants.personality: Gruff, territorial. Sees Garrick as competition. Garrison rates.

merchants.name: Senna Vael
merchants.personality: Meticulous, suspicious. Won't sell dangerous compounds without knowing buyer.

merchants.name: Old Corin
merchants.specialty: Miscellaneous. Occasional unusual items from south.
merchants.personality: Talkative, unreliable. Exaggerates. Prices vary by mood.

factions.name: Order of Dawn's Light
factions.description: Religious military order. Lathander. Founded 3 months ago. HQ Dawn's Rest. ~120 residents, 7 core combatants.

factions.name: Cult of Malar
factions.description: Thirty-year network. Three cells destroyed. Uses forced transformation and compulsion.

factions.name: Corvus Barony
factions.description: Semi-autonomous barony. Allied by treaty. Council divided.

factions.name: Temple of Torm (Millford)
factions.description: Local temple. Trained Jakob. Sent Mara to observe. Cautious.
goals: Assess if Order is ally or competitor

side_quests.title: Riverside Cell — Finn's Infiltration
side_quests.description: Finn's three-month recon on Sister Kara's urban cell. Balancing intelligence vs exposure, assault vs collateral.
side_quests.rewards: Cell destroyed. Deepwood intel. Finn proves himself.

side_quests.title: Rescue Elena Holloway
side_quests.description: Twelve-year-old werewolf held hostage. Eight years transformed. May not remember being human.
side_quests.rewards: Elena freed. Darius permanent ally. Mercy validated at highest stakes.

side_quests.title: Plague of Whispers
side_quests.description: Psychic plague near Darkwood. Side effect of Master's ritual. Mercy's limits when victims are dangerous.
side_quests.rewards: Understanding Master's ritual. Clue to true plan. Civilian trust.

side_quests.title: Gregor's Missing Patrol
side_quests.description: Eight soldiers captured with Gregor. Trail leads to Captain Vey — possible Fourth Circle.
side_quests.rewards: Patrol recovered. Fourth Circle exposed. Garrison security assessed.

side_quests.title: The Successor Question
side_quests.description: Who leads if Riv dies? No plan exists. Creating one forces mortality confrontation.
side_quests.rewards: Institutional resilience. Character growth. Order becomes larger than one person.

themes: Redemption and Mercy — defining principle, constantly tested
Found Family — every member orphaned, displaced, or broken
Limits of Idealism — wisdom about when mercy works and doesn't
Master and Apprentice — Riv/Jakob, Garrick/Emma, Dagmar/Finn
Light Against Darkness — Lathander vs Malar, literal and metaphorical
Leadership and Sacrifice — every decision risks lives
Cost of War — named characters die, victories have funerals

session_zero_topics: Post-time-skip reentry — three months passed offscreen
Icewind Dale expedition is immediate arc (2-4 sessions)
Party: Riv, Lyra, Jakob, Voss — small, intimate
Spiritual Crucible will strip and restore powers
Jakob changed during training — explore it
potential_twists: DM-ONLY: Master's Apotheosis reframes everything when discovered
DM-ONLY: Dawn's Rest consecration on a clock
DM-ONLY: Colony ruins not entirely dead
Gregor's Vey intel reveals garrison infiltration
Temple of Torm assessment creates friction
backup_hooks: Ignore Riverside: entrenches with political protection
dm_notes.tone_guidance: Serious with levity. Character-driven. Moral complexity. Found family tested not undermined. Collaborative. Real consequences with fair warnings.
encounter_design_principles: Every session: at least one moment where Riv's idealism is relevant
NPCs have own agendas. Ask: what does this person want independent of Riv?
World moves between sessions. Report through dialogue, letters, rumors, environment.
Seed threads early, pay off late. Let player connect dots.
Not every problem has combat solution. Political, economic, social, environmental.
Let player fail meaningfully. Some missions don't succeed.
Reward engagement with depth. Ignored threads develop consequences.
Keep tone grounded. Even Master has human moments. Allies have flaws. Victories cost.

spiritual_crucible._schema_extension: CUSTOM FIELD. Icewind Dale power degradation/restoration mechanic.
spiritual_crucible.overview: Residual Malar corruption strips divine power. Exploits unresolved internal conflicts. Power returns through self-confrontation, not rest. Narrate degradation, don't announce DCs.

phases.name: The Dimming
phases.effect: 3rd+ spells: DC 12 WIS or one level lower. Channel Divinity -25%. Non-divine: disadv WIS checks.
phases.narrative: Subtle faltering. Cure Wounds heals minimum. Prayers feel empty.

phases.name: The Stripping
phases.effect: 3rd+ spells: DC 15 WIS or fail (slot spent). Channel Div: DC 13 Religion. Non-divine: lose prof on WIS. ~Level 3-4 effectiveness.
phases.narrative: Cold gets inside thoughts. Intrusive reflections. Confrontation forced.

phases.name: Breakthrough
phases.effect: Power returns ALL AT ONCE on honest self-confrontation. Level choices happen here.
phases.narrative: Honesty breaks corruption. Light floods back. Magic overwhelms.

phases.name: Full Power
phases.effect: Restored. Possibly stronger. Level choices reflect lessons.

confrontations.riv: Why build Order? Noble: Lathander's promise. Real: boy never enough built something essential. Mercy makes people need him. Breakthrough: acknowledge complicated motivations, keep going anyway.
confrontations.jakob: Temple taught mercy without justice = weakness. Cold: 'Mercy let Silas live. Was that justice?' Breakthrough: can disagree with master and still love him.
confrontations.lyra: Village she didn't save. Chose contract. Cold: 'Would you now?' Breakthrough: admitting she doesn't know. Showing vulnerability.
confrontations.voss: Eleven civilians. Correct decision. Cold: 'What when the hard call comes again?' Breakthrough: decision was impossible not wrong. Stop self-punishment.

homebrew_systems._schema_extension: CUSTOM FIELD. Active homebrew mechanics beyond standard 5e.

heat_tracker.description: Hidden. Cult attention on Order. 0-10. Increases: +2 raid, +3 kill leadership, +1 public/rescue. Decreases: -1 lay low, -2 disinfo. At 6+: retaliation rolls.

glory_points.description: Max 3. Earned: exceptional RP, tactics, sacrifice. Spend: 1=reroll, 2=auto-success, 3=dramatic survival.

shadow_points.description: Moral corruption. Current: 0. Effects scale 0(none) to 11+(deity withdraws).

relationship_levels.description: 1-5. Gates NPC disclosure. 1=acquaintance, 2=friendly(+favors), 3=close(+1 rolls), 4=deep bond(risk life), 5=legendary.

stronghold_economy.description: Monthly income/expenses. Modest surplus. Actions: 1/month (Intel, Train, Fortify, Craft, Diplomacy, Rest).

injury_table.description: d10 on dropping to 0 HP. 1-6 minor, 7-9 major, 10 grievous.

exhaustion.description: Revised gradual: -1 to -5 all d20, speed/HP reductions. Level 6=unconscious.

mass_combat.description: Morale 10 base. Modifiers for commander/victories/casualties/terror. Check at 25% losses.

mythic_progression.description: Post-Level 20 framework. 5 tiers (Touched by Legend → Apotheosis). Earned through narrative, not XP. Paths: Hierophant (Riv), Beast (Master), Champion (Lyra/Garrick/Voss), Trickster (Finn), Legend (Jakob), Redemption (Silas/Thane), Corrupted Dawn (Riv dark mirror). Not active Campaign I. Full details in top-level mythic_progression field.

oath_of_atonement._schema_extension: CUSTOM FIELD. Formal oath for inducting redeemed cultists.
oath_of_atonement.summary: Five pledges: service to Order, repentance for crimes (daily prayer, house arrest), accept rules/leadership, surrender all cult knowledge, understand betrayal = death and erasure. Final: accept assigned role.

cult_cell_status._schema_extension: CUSTOM FIELD. Tracks all known cult operational points.

cells.name: Thornhaven Cell
cells.details: Gareth killed, Korthak killed at Battle of Thornhaven, 15+ werewolves eliminated.

cells.name: Ashford Cell
cells.details: 8 cultists, 2 werewolves. Brennan captured and redeemed.

cells.name: Northwood Cell
cells.details: 11 cultists killed, 3 werewolves, 5 captured. Thane, Petrik, Willem freed. Darius extracted. Documents seized.

cells.name: Riverside Cell
cells.details: Warehouse district near Millford docks. 2+ werewolves, 2-4 cultists. Urban — complicates assault. Finn infiltration planned.

cells.name: Deepwood Cell
cells.details: 20-25 veteran cultists, 10-15 werewolves, professional fortifications. Elena Holloway hostage. Primary target. Baron's one-year timeline.

cells.name: Westmarch Safe House
cells.details: "Mallory's Provisions," warehouse district, 2 permanent staff. Logistics node. Intelligence value.

campaign_history._schema_extension: CUSTOM FIELD. Key historical events the bot must know for continuity.
campaign_history.riv_backstory: Born 1301 DR Neverwinter. Father: cold magistrate. Mother: grieving, never recovered from eldest daughter lost to bandits. Younger sister (bard) received all attention. Riv found Lathander at ~20, pledged to found an Order. Wandered six years as traveling cleric.
key_dead_characters: Marcus Ironwood — Garrick's apprentice/adopted son. Died Battle of Thornhaven age 23. Killed four werewolves, kept fighting with broken sword.
Korthak — Alpha werewolf. Twenty years Malar service by choice. Killed at Battle of Thornhaven.
Gareth — Werewolf lieutenant. Captured/tormented Aldric. Killed in cave assault.
Jakob's family — Parents, sister Sara, brother Tomlin, Uncle Gareth, Aunt Mira, cousin Petya. All killed by werewolves.
campaign_history.shield_of_the_fallen: Memorial at Dawn's Rest. Sixteen names. Eight defenders died at Battle of Thornhaven plus other casualties.
campaign_history.universal_oath_ceremony: Day 17. Garrick forged nine silver rings. Every core member pledged. The moment the Order became an institution.
campaign_history.thorn_guard: Lyra's former twenty-person mercenary company. Seventeen-year record: never lost client, cargo, or guard. Disbanded/folded into Order.

mythic_progression._schema_extension: CUSTOM FIELD. Post-Level 20 progression system adapted from Pathfinder Mythic Adventures / Wrath of the Righteous.
mythic_progression.status: Framework only. Not active in Campaign I. Reserved for future campaigns when characters reach Level 20+.
sources: Pathfinder: Wrath of the Righteous (Mythic Paths, video game adaptation)
design_principles: 5 tiers (condensed from PF's 10) to fit 5E bounded accuracy
Earned through narrative trials, NOT XP
Mythic abilities are qualitative (new capabilities) not just quantitative (+5 to everything)
Mythic Power pool replaces per-tier uses
Shadow Points interact: Light paths require Shadow 0-2, Dark paths gained through Shadow accumulation
Tier 3+ affects Relationship dynamics — mortals intimidated by demigod presence
Master's exact abilities remain DM secret until revealed in-game

tiers.name: Touched by Legend
tiers.description: First mythic awakening. Minor supernatural enhancements.

tiers.name: Champion
tiers.description: National fame. Shaping events through presence.

1: Divine Surge (3/day, +1d6 radiant to any roll)
Dawn's Blessing (long rest restores extra hit dice = WIS mod)
2: Lathander's Chosen (1/day cast any Cleric spell without slot, upcast to max)
Beacon of Hope (60ft aura, dying stabilize, healing +50%)
3: Avatar of Dawn (10min 1/LR: fly 60ft, immune radiant/necrotic, +3d8 radiant)
Mass Restoration (Greater Restoration on 6 targets simultaneously)
4: Resurrection Mastery (True Resurrection at will, no components)
Solar Judgment (60ft radius 10d10 radiant, DC 20 WIS, undead/fiends no save)
5: Lathander's Herald (quasi-deity, grant divine power to followers)
Reshape Dawn (1/year rewrite single event within last 24 hours)

beast_dark_path.description: DM-ONLY. Dark mirror to Hierophant. 40+ year evolution.

tier_abilities.1: Master werewolf — voice control of pack, no totems needed
tier_abilities.2: Malar's favored — cult leader, divine dark blessing
tier_abilities.3: Apex predator — enhanced lycanthropy, regeneration, terror aura
tier_abilities.4: Dark champion — mythic vs mythic equality with Riv
tier_abilities.5: Beast-God — lesser deity of hunt/slaughter if not stopped

champion.description: Martial excellence. Perfect strikes, legendary resilience, impossible strength, army leadership, training successors.

marshal.description: Leadership genius. Not divine power but tactical brilliance + inspirational presence. Coordinate dozens, grant army-wide bonuses, strategic precognition.

trickster.description: Perfect deception, reality-bending disguises, being in two places simultaneously, making armies forget he exists.

legend.description: Rejecting divine power for pure mortal excellence. Level 20 Cleric WITHOUT mythic supernatural boost but with perfect execution. Philosophically fascinating for a Cleric rejecting divinity's shortcuts.

redemption.description: Custom path — not in source material. Mythic through atonement, industrial-scale compulsion breaking, institution building. Tier 5: Living Saint of Second Chances — sense and break corruption/compulsion through touch.

corrupted_dawn.description: What if Riv falls? Dominate Person at will on sworn followers, radiant becomes necrotic, healing becomes control, gain power from breaking vows, control lycanthropes like The Master. Narrative cost: everything.

multi_campaign_timeline.campaign_1: No mythic. Levels 6-15. Foundation. Master may be Tier 1 (unrevealed). Mortal vs mortal.
multi_campaign_timeline.campaign_2: Levels 15-20. Order expands. Lathander offers ascension. Riv achieves Tier 1-2 Hierophant. Master returns at Tier 2 (if escaped).
multi_campaign_timeline.campaign_3: Decades later. Riv Tier 3-4. Multiple party members mythic (Jakob T1-2, Lyra T1, Silas T1 Redemption). Mythic heroes vs mythic villains.
multi_campaign_timeline.campaign_4: Final. Tier 5 apotheosis decisions. Riv chooses: godhood, legendary mortality, or sacrificial transcendence. Order's ultimate fate. Epilogue centuries later.

bounded_accuracy_solutions: Mythic bonuses apply to damage/healing more than attack rolls
Opposed mythic checks (Tier 3 vs Tier 3 = mythic pool vs mythic pool, normal NPCs irrelevant)
Legendary resistance uses Mythic Power (spend 1 point = auto-succeed)

homebrew_integration.shadow_points: Mythic amplifies corruption. Dark path = accumulate Shadow. Light path requires Shadow 0-2.
homebrew_integration.relationships: Tier 3+ makes mortal relationships harder. Must work to stay grounded.
homebrew_integration.exhaustion: Tier 1-2 still vulnerable. Tier 3+ immune to mundane exhaustion but mythic exhaustion possible.
homebrew_integration.equipment: Tier 3+ needs legendary/artifact gear to match power level.
